# Twin studies highlight the role of genes in school success

[Interviews](https://boldscience.org/interviews/) [Videos](https://boldscience.org/videos/) [Development](https://boldscience.org/development/) [Genetics](https://boldscience.org/topics/genetics/) [Neuroscience](https://boldscience.org/topics/neuroscience/)

13 February 2017

Play video

Close Video

### Interview by:

### [Cathrin Jerie](https://boldscience.org/authors/cathrin-jerie/)

Program Manager at Jacobs Foundation

Dorret I. Boomsma, professor of biological psychology at the Vrije Universiteit Amsterdam has studied the genes of twins for over 30 years and has found that DNA variants have a predictive power in educational attainment.

### Interview by:

### [Cathrin Jerie](https://boldscience.org/authors/cathrin-jerie/)

Program Manager at Jacobs Foundation
